purpose: Comparison of cost, tokens, and latency before and after caching integration for AIE1 product-review server.
method: Comparative evaluation of 6 normal cases running sequentially on a local server. Baseline is compared with Cold Cache (first run) and Hot Cache (subsequent cache hit runs).

baseline_before_cache.description: No cache integrated. All requests call Bedrock (Nova Lite + Nova Micro).

cold_cache.description: First run after caching integration. Part of the cases hit the cache (from partial previous tests) and the rest call Bedrock to populate the cache.

hot_cache.description: Subsequent run. 5 out of 6 cases hit the Redis cache directly. Case 1 (unverified summary) is bypassed by the Cache Policy and calls Bedrock.

key_findings.latency_improvement: P50 latency decreased from 2.8213 seconds to 0.0044 seconds (4.4 milliseconds) on Hot Cache, yielding a 641x speedup.
key_findings.cost_savings: Bedrock API calls were reduced from 12 to 2 (83.3% reduction in cost and token usage) for Hot Cache runs because cached responses consume zero Bedrock tokens.
key_findings.cache_policy_fidelity: The Cache Policy successfully prevented caching of unverified responses (Case 1), ensuring that incorrect summaries are always re-evaluated rather than served from cache.
key_findings.concurrency_safeguard: Distributed locks (SET NX EX 10) protect the system from Cache Stampede (Thundering Herd) when multiple requests hit the same missing cache key simultaneously.
